description: Create a harness checkpoint for the stale Telegram `/coding status` mismatch repair task.
steps: Write PLAN.json and PROGRESS.md for this scoped bugfix.
Record the existing pytest baseline and the observed stale task record.
Verify the scaffold can be committed independently of unrelated worktree changes.

description: Reproduce the mismatch using the shared coding-task workspace store instead of the repository-local tree.
steps: Resolve nanobot's active workspace from runtime config.
Inspect `automation/coding/tasks.json` under that workspace.
Confirm the selected Telegram task points at a repo whose current harness state no longer matches the stored conflict reason.

description: Define the source-of-truth rule for stale harness-conflict placeholder tasks.
steps: Treat the repository's current harness files as authoritative.
Identify which task statuses and metadata combinations are safe to auto-clear.
Keep the rule narrow enough to avoid touching genuine running work.

description: Implement stale harness-conflict detection in the coding-task policy layer.
steps: Inspect waiting_user tasks whose metadata contains a harness conflict reason.
Compare the stored expectation against `detect_repo_harness(repo_path)`.
Prepare those stale placeholders to be cleared before selection or blocking decisions.

description: Automatically clear stale placeholder records when `/coding` control flows encounter them.
steps: Use the actual repository state to determine whether the placeholder is stale.
Transition the stale placeholder out of the visible control set with a clear audit summary.
Ensure `/coding status`, `/coding list`, and new `/coding` starts no longer select or block on that record.

description: Preserve valid waiting states such as genuine harness conflicts and worker-exit review waits.
steps: Leave matching `repo_active_harness` and `repo_completed_harness` placeholders intact.
Keep `worker_exit_review` waits non-blocking but still visible when they are the real current task.
Avoid changing unrelated running, failed, completed, or cancelled tasks.

description: Add regression coverage for status selection when a stale conflict record is present.
steps: Create a stale waiting_user conflict task in a temporary repo without matching harness files.
Issue `/coding status` from Telegram private chat.
Assert the stale record is cleared and the user sees the no-manageable-task response.

description: Add regression coverage for new-task startup when a stale conflict record is present.
steps: Seed a stale waiting_user conflict task for one repo.
Start a new `/coding <repo> <goal>` task for another repo.
Assert the stale record no longer blocks creation or launch.

description: Verify that list/index selection also ignores stale conflict placeholders.
steps: Exercise policy-level visible task enumeration with stale placeholders.
Confirm visible task indexing excludes the cleared record.
Check that no valid visible task disappears as a side effect.

description: Run targeted automated verification for the policy and Telegram routing changes.
steps: Run focused pytest coverage for `tests/coding_tasks/test_policy.py` and the relevant Telegram routing tests.
Confirm the new assertions pass.
Record any unrelated pre-existing failures separately from this task.

description: Checkpoint the implemented repair in git without sweeping in unrelated user worktree changes.
steps: Stage only the files created or modified for this task.
Create a non-interactive commit with the required Codex trailer.
Keep unrelated edits in other files untouched.

description: Clean up harness scaffolding once every scoped repair item is complete.
steps: Append a completion note to PROGRESS.md with the verification outcome.
Remove PLAN.json and PROGRESS.md after all items pass.
Create the final cleanup commit so the repo is left with code changes only.
